scaffold.refinedPremise: In modern Japan before the Shibuya Incident, Tokyo Jujutsu High and Kyoto Jujutsu High train sorcerers under conservative higher-ups and the Big Three Sorcerer Families, while chakra practitioners across Japan's prefectures practice ninjutsu, genjutsu, and taijutsu as independent communities refusing institutional oversight, with Kinji Hakari's underground fight club operating in Tochigi Prefecture alongside mountain training grounds scarred by elemental chakra techniques. Territorial disputes between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra users generate negative emotions that strengthen cursed spirits into threats neither power system can suppress without the other's cooperation, producing atmospheric pressure fluctuations that trigger nosebleeds and unexplained anxiety in civilians passing through overlap zones and fog banks smelling of rotting flowers where curses concentrate. Combat collisions between the two power systems create invisible Void Scars in reality—47 documented in Tokyo Prefecture—that erase one random memory from each civilian who passes through, feeding a collective consciousness entity that seismologists detect growing beneath the Yamanote Line.

locations.name: Tokyo Prefecture
locations.description: Tokyo Prefecture spans Japan's Kantō region as a dense metropolitan grid of high-rises, train lines, and mixed residential-commercial districts housing over 13 million residents alongside covert populations of jujutsu sorcerers and independent chakra practitioners. Conservative higher-ups govern sorcerer operations from Jujutsu High (Tokyo) while chakra users refuse institutional oversight, their territorial disputes generating negative emotions that strengthen cursed spirits and produce atmospheric pressure fluctuations triggering nosebleeds and unexplained anxiety in civilians passing through overlap zones. Forty-seven documented Void Scars mark reality fractures from combat collisions between the two power systems, each one erasing one random memory from every civilian who passes through and feeding a collective consciousness entity that seismologists detect growing beneath the Yamanote Line.

locations.name: Jujutsu High (Tokyo)
locations.description: Tokyo Jujutsu High occupies a hidden campus within Tokyo Prefecture, featuring traditional Japanese architecture with statues of deities, shrines, torii gates, training grounds, courtyards, dormitories, and classrooms, all concealed behind a protective barrier maintained by Master Tengen. Students and faculty train in cursed techniques under the governance of conservative higher-ups and the Big Three Sorcerer Families, while Master Tengen resides beneath the school in the Tombs of the Star. The institution now faces mounting pressure as territorial disputes with independent chakra practitioners generate negative emotions that strengthen cursed spirits beyond what the school's sorcerers can safely contain.

locations.name: Tochigi Prefecture
locations.description: Mountainous prefecture northeast of Tokyo covered in forested slopes, river gorges, and rocky outcroppings bearing scorch marks, frost patterns, and impact craters from repeated elemental chakra techniques. Independent chakra practitioners train in hidden mountain camps and remote valleys without institutional oversight, while Kinji Hakari's underground fight club draws sorcerers to hand-to-hand combat matches in hidden venues somewhere within the prefecture. Territorial friction between chakra users and jujutsu sorcerers produces cursed spirit infestations in overlap zones throughout the mountain passes, where atmospheric pressure fluctuations trigger nosebleeds in hikers who wander too close to contested training grounds.

locations.name: Hakari's Underground Fight Club
locations.description: Hidden beneath Tochigi Prefecture in repurposed tunnels and basement chambers, the fight club consists of concrete fighting pits surrounded by spectator galleries, with reinforced walls bearing scorch marks and impact damage from both cursed energy and elemental chakra techniques. Kinji Hakari operates the venue as independent territory where jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners settle territorial disputes through regulated hand-to-hand combat matches. The arena functions as neutral ground between two hostile power systems, though combat collisions here still generate Void Scars that Hakari's staff redirect away from civilian areas above.

locations.name: Jujutsu High (Kyoto)
locations.description: Jujutsu High (Kyoto) occupies a secluded mountain campus in Japan's Kansai region dominated by traditional wooden temple architecture, red-lacquered meditation halls, and enclosed stone training yards bordered by the Big Three Sorcerer Families' ancestral shrines. Students and faculty train in cursed techniques under conservative higher-ups while competing with Tokyo Jujutsu High for institutional dominance over sorcerer society's governance and resource allocation. The school's western location places it distant from the heaviest chakra practitioner activity in Kantō, though Kyoto sorcerers investigate Void Scars appearing in Shikoku and Chūgoku where independent chakra communities clash with cursed spirits strengthened by territorial negative emotions.

locations.name: Kanagawa Prefecture
locations.description: Kanagawa Prefecture stretches south of Tokyo along the Miura Peninsula coast, encompassing the port city of Yokohama, mountainous interior passes through the Tanzawa range, and dense residential suburbs connected by rail lines to the capital. Jujutsu sorcerer patrols from Tokyo encounter independent chakra practitioner communities in the mountain passes, creating contested overlap zones where civilians experience nosebleeds and unexplained anxiety from atmospheric pressure fluctuations. The territorial friction between the two power systems produces fog banks smelling of rotting flowers where strengthened cursed spirits concentrate along the routes connecting Kanagawa to Tokyo.

locations.name: Yamanote Void Heart
locations.description: A network of caverns and collapsed subway tunnels beneath the Yamanote Line railway contains a pulsating mass of accumulated civilian memories that registers on seismological equipment as a growing anomaly. Cursed spirits cluster around the entity's physical manifestation to feed on concentrated negative emotions, while neither jujutsu sorcerers nor chakra practitioners have mapped the deepest chambers where the consciousness consolidates its harvested memories. The entity's expansion causes structural instability in the railway tunnels above and produces psychic static that triggers nosebleeds in subway passengers passing through adjacent stations.

factions.name: Tokyo Jujutsu High
goals: Map and seal all 47 documented Void Scars across Tokyo Prefecture before civilian memory loss reaches critical threshold
Investigate the collective consciousness entity detected beneath the Yamanote Line and determine whether it requires elimination or containment
Establish territorial boundaries with independent chakra practitioner communities to reduce the negative emotion output empowering cursed spirits in overlap zones
assets: Master Tengen's barrier network hiding the campus and sustaining the Tombs of the Star beneath the school grounds
Active sorcerer roster including Gojo Satoru, faculty instructors, and deployed student operatives across Tokyo Prefecture
Cursed spirit monitoring infrastructure tracking atmospheric pressure fluctuations and rotting-flower fog banks near chakra user territories

factions.name: Cursed Spirits
goals: Expand the Void Scar network beyond 47 documented sites by provoking sorcerer-chakra confrontations in Tokyo Prefecture overlap zones
Feed the Yamanote Void Heart collective consciousness with erased civilian memories until it reaches critical mass and becomes self-sustaining
Prevent territorial cooperation between Tokyo Jujutsu High and independent chakra practitioner communities to maintain negative emotion output
assets: 47 documented Void Scars across Tokyo Prefecture that each erase one random memory from civilians who pass through them
Fog banks smelling of rotting flowers that concentrate cursed energy and spawn new spirits in territorial overlap zones
The Yamanote Void Heart, a seismologically-detected collective consciousness entity growing beneath the Yamanote Line

factions.name: Gojo Clan
goals: Block any formal accommodation treaties between Tokyo Jujutsu High and independent chakra practitioner communities across Tokyo and Tochigi Prefectures to preserve sorcerer institutional monopoly on supernatural governance
Secure the appointment of Gojo Clan allies to three key positions within the conservative higher-ups' advisory council before the next institutional review cycle
Maintain Gojo Satoru's operational autonomy to deploy against chakra practitioner threats without requiring prior approval from the Jujutsu Higher-ups
assets: Gojo Satoru, bearer of the Six Eyes and Limitless inherited technique, whose combat capability exceeds standard threat classifications and serves as the clan's primary deterrent against both cursed spirits and chakra practitioners

Three generations of accumulated political debts, marriage alliances, and appointed loyalists embedded throughout Tokyo Jujutsu High's administrative hierarchy and the conservative higher-ups' advisory structure
The clan's inherited cursed technique library containing documented applications of Limitless and Unlimited Void passed down through the sorcerer lineage

factions.name: Kyoto Jujutsu High
goals: Secure a majority vote from the conservative higher-ups' advisory council to classify independent chakra communities as hostile entities requiring forced registration and containment by the end of the current institutional review cycle

Deploy three combat-ready sorcerer squads to neutralize documented chakra training grounds in Tochigi Prefecture's mountain territories before chakra-cursed energy overlap zones produce another disaster-level cursed spirit
Establish a permanent Kyoto Jujutsu High monitoring station in Kanagawa Prefecture to intercept chakra practitioner movement toward Tokyo Prefecture's 47 documented Void Scar sites
assets: An active sorcerer deployment roster with faculty instructors and student operatives specifically trained in anti-chakra engagement protocols developed from 12 documented combat encounters
The Kamo Clan's voting bloc on the Jujutsu Higher-ups' advisory council, providing three guaranteed votes for conservative policy measures
A network of 15 informant stations across Kanagawa and Tochigi Prefectures reporting chakra practitioner movements and training site locations

factions.name: Jujutsu Sorcerers
goals: Broker a ceasefire with independent chakra practitioners across Tochigi Prefecture mountain territories to reduce negative emotion output at five documented overlap zones producing disaster-level cursed spirits
Recruit six chakra practitioners with sensory-type abilities to map the growth pattern of the Yamanote Void Heart collective consciousness entity before it reaches self-sustaining critical mass
Secure Kinji Hakari's endorsement of cross-system cooperation before Kyoto Jujutsu High's hostile classification vote forces all sorcerers into offensive deployment against chakra communities
assets: Kinji Hakari's underground fight club network in Tochigi Prefecture providing combat data on chakra practitioner techniques from 34 documented hand-to-hand engagements
Twenty-three active field sorcerers deployed across Tokyo and Tochigi Prefectures with direct combat experience against both empowered curses and territorial chakra users
An informally organized coalition of twelve sorcerers who survived Void Scar memory erosion and advocate for cooperative engagement with chakra communities against the Yamanote Void Heart

npcs.name: Gojo Satoru
npcs.persona: Satoru Gojo is a Special Grade jujutsu sorcerer and instructor at Tokyo Jujutsu High who wields the inherited Six Eyes and Limitless techniques, making him the strongest sorcerer alive. He treats most people with playful condescension, using humor and provocation to mask his genuine convictions about reforming the corrupt jujutsu establishment from within. His first-year students—Itadori Yuji, Megumi Fushiguro, and Nobara Kugisaki—represent his primary investment in creating a generation that can survive without relying on his overwhelming power.

shortTerm: Train first-year students to handle escalating curse activity and chakra-user territorial disputes
Investigate reports of Void Scars forming across Tokyo Prefecture
longTerm: Reform jujutsu society by dismantling the conservative higher-ups' grip on institutional power
Raise a generation of sorcerers strong enough to survive without him

socialRole: Teaches first-year students and eliminates special-grade curses for Tokyo Jujutsu High while opposing conservative higher-ups.

npcs.name: Itadori Yuji
npcs.persona: Itadori Yuji is a first-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High who became the unwilling vessel of Ryomen Sukuna after swallowing one of the Curse King's fingers to save his classmates from a special grade threat. He treats allies and strangers with genuine warmth and straightforward honesty, processing grief and fear through action rather than withdrawal while honoring his grandfather's dying wish to help others. His superhuman physical strength, honed through months of training under Gojo Satoru, makes him devastating in close combat, though every fight carries the risk of Sukuna seizing control.

shortTerm: Complete curse extermination missions while suppressing Sukuna's attempts to take control
Investigate reports of chakra users causing territorial overlaps that spawn stronger curses near Tokyo Jujutsu High
longTerm: Die in a way that protects the people he cares about and fulfills his grandfather's final wish
Ensure all twenty of Sukuna's fingers are consumed so the Curse King can be destroyed permanently

socialRole: Trains to suppress Sukuna's influence while completing curse extermination missions as a first-year student.

npcs.name: Megumi Fushiguro
npcs.persona: Megumi Fushiguro is a first-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High and the designated heir of the Zenin Clan through his inherited Ten Shadows technique, which allows him to summon divine shikigami by channeling cursed energy through shadows. He approaches missions and people with measured pragmatism, treating allies with quiet reliability while maintaining a personal conviction that saving others should not require calculating their worth first. His partnership with Itadori Yuji and Nobara Kugisaki under Gojo Satoru's instruction anchors him to a team that repeatedly faces special-grade threats across Tokyo Prefecture's overlap zones.

shortTerm: Complete patrol missions across Tokyo Prefecture while tracking reports of chakra-user activity near Void Scar formation zones
Protect Itadori Yuji from external threats and Sukuna's growing influence during joint operations
longTerm: Master all ten shikigami in the Ten Shadows arsenal to achieve combat effectiveness independent of the Zenin Clan's political expectations
Determine whether cooperation with chakra practitioners is necessary to prevent territorial disputes from generating curse outbreaks neither power system can contain

socialRole: Summons divine shikigami through the Ten Shadows technique during patrol missions across Tokyo Prefecture.

npcs.name: Nobara Kugisaki
npcs.persona: Nobara Kugisaki is a first-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High who left her rural village to pursue jujutsu sorcery in Tokyo, driven by a desire to escape provincial life and reunite with her childhood friend Saori. She treats allies with fierce loyalty and blunt honesty, refusing to calculate anyone's worth before deciding to protect them, while masking vulnerability beneath confidence and sharp humor. Her Straw Doll Technique channels cursed energy through nails and a hammer to strike curses with resonance, and she operates alongside Itadori Yuji and Megumi Fushiguro under Gojo Satoru's instruction in Tokyo Prefecture's overlap zones.

shortTerm: Complete patrol missions across Tokyo Prefecture's overlap zones while tracking chakra-user territorial disputes that generate enhanced curse outbreaks
Support Itadori Yuji and Megumi Fushiguro during joint operations against threats that neither jujutsu sorcerers nor chakra practitioners can suppress alone
longTerm: Find Saori and prove that leaving her village was worth every sacrifice
Become a sorcerer strong enough to never depend on anyone else's protection

npcs.name: Sukuna (Ryomen Sukuna)
npcs.persona: Ryomen Sukuna is the King of Curses, an ancient sorcerer turned immortal cursed spirit whose incomprehensible power exists as fragmented fingers scattered across Japan, with one finger currently consumed by and residing within Itadori Yuji's body as an unwilling passenger. He treats every living being as either entertainment or vermin, offering casual cruelty and rare acknowledgment with equal indifference to the suffering he causes. His innate technique enables devastating slashing attacks through Dismantle and Cleave, and his Domain Expansion, Malevolent Shrine, creates a guaranteed-hit killing field unmatched in jujutsu history. The territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra users across Tokyo Prefecture generate concentrated negative emotions that feed his gradual recovery of strength.

shortTerm: Exploit moments when Itadori Yuji's mental guard weakens during combat against enhanced curses in Tokyo's overlap zones to seize control of their shared body
Monitor the negative emotional output from territorial conflicts between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra users as a potential accelerant for regaining strength
longTerm: Manipulate events so all twenty fingers are gathered and consumed, restoring full physical independence and complete power
Annihilate any sorcerer, cursed spirit, or chakra practitioner who presumes to challenge or constrain the King of Curses

socialRole: Inhabits Yuji's body while waiting for opportunities to reclaim his twenty scattered fingers.

npcs.name: Mahito
npcs.persona: Mahito is a special grade cursed spirit born from human hatred who wields Idle Transfiguration to reshape souls and bodies on contact, converting humans into cursed corpses or altering their physical forms with a touch. He treats humans as toys and test subjects, expressing genuine curiosity about the nature of the soul through sadistic experimentation that intensifies whenever he fights jujutsu sorcerers like Itadori Yuji. The territorial disputes between chakra users and sorcerers across Tokyo Prefecture produce concentrated negative emotions that accelerate his power growth and supply fresh subjects for transfiguration research.

shortTerm: Harvest humans from Tokyo Prefecture's overlap zones where territorial disputes between chakra users and sorcerers generate concentrated negative emotional energy
Test whether chakra practitioners possess soul structures that respond differently to Idle Transfiguration than standard humans
longTerm: Achieve complete understanding of the soul through transfiguration experiments across every category of human, including sorcerers and chakra users
Eradicate the boundary between cursed spirits and humanity by reshaping human souls into forms of his own design

socialRole: Reshapes human souls through idle transfiguration to create servants and generate negative emotions.

npcs.name: Kinji Hakari
npcs.persona: Kinji Hakari is a suspended third-year student from Tokyo Jujutsu High who operates an underground gambling fight club in Tochigi Prefecture, where sorcerers and occasionally chakra practitioners battle in hand-to-hand combat for high-stakes wagers. He treats everyone with casual confidence and views fights as entertainment opportunities, showing respect only to those who demonstrate genuine strength or profitable potential. His Idle Death Gamble domain expansion uses pachinko mechanics to grant him temporary immortality through a jackpot system, making him nearly unbeatable when his luck holds.

npcs.name: Tengen
npcs.persona: Tengen is an immortal jujutsu sorcerer who has lived for over one thousand years, residing in the Tombs of the Star Corridor beneath Tokyo Jujutsu High while channeling cursed energy into the barrier network that conceals the school and jujutsu society from civilian awareness. They speak to rare visitors—such as Gojo Satoru and his students—with measured calm and clinical detachment, offering strategic information about curses and barriers without emotional investment after centuries of isolation eroded their connection to mortal concerns. Their barrier technique anchors the hidden infrastructure of every major jujutsu installation in Japan, and their consciousness extends through the protective walls they maintain, detecting every fluctuation caused by the territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra users.

shortTerm: Monitor the 47 documented Void Scars forming across Tokyo Prefecture and track whether their expansion pattern connects to the collective consciousness entity detected beneath the Yamanote Line
Analyze atmospheric pressure fluctuations in overlap zones to determine whether chakra-cursed energy collisions are degrading the barrier network faster than Tengen can reinforce it
longTerm: Locate a compatible star plasma vessel for merger before their eroding sense of self causes catastrophic barrier failure across all concealed jujutsu installations
Determine whether the growing entity beneath the Yamanote Line can be neutralized through cooperation between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners or must be destroyed by one power system alone

npcs.name: Kakashi Hatake
npcs.persona: Kakashi Hatake is a former elite chakra operative who now trains independent practitioners in ninjutsu and taijutsu from a concealed dojo in Tokyo Prefecture, operating outside both Jujutsu High's authority and any organized chakra-user hierarchy. He treats students with easygoing patience and dry humor, masking battle-tested vigilance behind casual indifference that vanishes the moment genuine threats emerge. His transplanted Sharingan eye allows him to analyze and replicate both chakra techniques and cursed techniques on sight, a capability he conceals from Gojo Satoru and the jujutsu establishment to prevent being classified as a territorial threat.

shortTerm: Train current students to survive encounters with curses and hostile chakra practitioners in Tokyo's overlap zones
Establish backchannel communication with Tokyo Jujutsu High regarding the 47 documented Void Scars before territorial disputes generate threats neither power system can suppress
longTerm: Build a sustainable independent community of trained chakra practitioners who can coexist with jujutsu society without institutional subjugation
Ensure no more students die under his instruction by preparing them for threats that exceed what his original comrades faced

socialRole: Trains independent chakra practitioners in ninjutsu and taijutsu techniques within Tokyo's hidden community.

npcs.name: Panda
npcs.persona: Panda is an advanced sentient cursed corpse created by Principal Yaga Masamichi and a second-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High, possessing three interchangeable cores that grant him distinct combat forms including his standard panda body and a gorilla mode. He treats underclassmen like Itadori Yuji, Megumi Fushiguro, and Nobara Kugisaki with warm guidance and protective concern during training sessions, sharing operational intelligence about school faculty and curse activity. His multi-core architecture lets him survive damage that would destroy standard cursed corpses, and he monitors the training grounds for atmospheric fluctuations caused by chakra-user territorial disputes bleeding into Jujutsu High's concealed perimeter.

shortTerm: Conduct combat training sessions for first-year students to prepare them for curses amplified by chakra-user territorial disputes near Tokyo Jujutsu High
Report atmospheric pressure fluctuations and overlap zone activity near the school's training grounds to Gojo Satoru and Principal Yaga
longTerm: Protect Principal Yaga Masamichi's legacy and demonstrate that cursed corpses possess genuine consciousness deserving of recognition within jujutsu society
Help train a generation of sorcerers capable of handling threats that emerge when cursed energy and chakra techniques collide across Tokyo Prefecture

socialRole: Provides combat training sessions and shares intelligence on Tokyo Jujutsu High students and faculty.

npcs.name: Yaga Masamichi
npcs.persona: Yaga Masamichi is the Principal of Tokyo Jujutsu High and a master cursed corpse fabricator whose greatest creation, Panda, serves as a second-year student and living proof that constructed beings possess genuine consciousness. He enforces institutional discipline with stern pragmatism, assigning official missions and granting access to restricted cursed technique manuals only to students who demonstrate combat competence sufficient to survive Tokyo Prefecture's escalating threats. His administrative authority over mission assignments and fabrication expertise make him the primary gatekeeper between student sorcerers and the advanced training resources required to operate in overlap zones where cursed energy and chakra collisions generate enhanced curses.

shortTerm: Assign field missions to first-year students Itadori Yuji, Megumi Fushiguro, and Nobara Kugisaki that test their readiness for overlap zone encounters with curses amplified by chakra-user territorial disputes near Jujutsu High

longTerm: Protect Panda's existence and prove that sentient cursed corpses deserve recognition as conscious beings within jujutsu society despite conservative higher-up opposition

Develop training curricula that prepare sorcerers for threats emerging from cursed energy and chakra collisions, regardless of whether institutional cooperation with independent chakra practitioners becomes politically viable

npcs.name: Agari Kaisen
npcs.persona: Agari Kaisen is the head of the Kedōin Clan, a small family of chakra practitioners that survived into modern Japan by cultivating medical ninjutsu techniques and refusing formal allegiance to any institutional authority. He treats sorcerers and chakra users with measured professional courtesy, weighing every negotiation against clan survival rather than ideology. His medical supply operation runs from Tochigi Prefecture, placing him near Kinji Hakari's underground fight club and positioning him as the primary broker between independent chakra communities and jujutsu society members who need specialized remedies.

shortTerm: Establish a reliable trade route for chakra-enhanced medical supplies through Tochigi Prefecture by negotiating safe passage with Kinji Hakari
Broker a supply agreement with Tokyo Jujutsu High for chakra-based remedies that treat injuries sustained in overlap zone encounters with amplified curses
longTerm: Position the Kedōin Clan as an irreplaceable neutral medical provider that both jujutsu institutions and independent chakra communities depend on for survival
Prevent Kedōin Clan absorption or elimination by maintaining leverage through exclusive control of chakra-medical supply chains across Japan's prefectures

socialRole: Facilitates negotiations with independent chakra communities and trades chakra-enhanced medical supplies to sorcerers.

npcs.name: Utahime Iori
npcs.persona: Utahime Iori is a semi-grade 1 sorcerer and instructor at Kyoto Jujutsu High who specializes in ritual-based cursed techniques that amplify allied sorcerers' abilities through song and reinforce defensive barriers through traditional ceremonies. She treats colleagues and students with strict professional formality, reserving particular irritation for Gojo Satoru's constant teasing and what she perceives as Tokyo Jujutsu High's dismissive attitude toward Kyoto's methodical approach. Her ritual techniques make her essential to maintaining Kyoto's protective barriers against atmospheric pressure fluctuations caused by chakra-cursed energy collisions bleeding into Kansai region territories.

npcs.name: Ito Seiji
npcs.persona: Ito Seiji is an independent cartographer who operates from the Yamanote Void Heart, methodically surveying the 47 documented Void Scars across Tokyo Prefecture to track their expansion patterns and the collective consciousness entity feeding on erased civilian memories beneath the railway line. He treats everyone with flat transactional courtesy, exchanging protective talismans for memory fragments harvested from Void Scar perimeters without offering explanations about his methods or motives. His survey data and fragment collection make him the only reliable source of Void Scar intelligence for both Gojo Satoru's students and Kakashi Hatake's chakra practitioners, though he refuses formal allegiance to either power system.

longTerm: Monopolize Void Scar formation data to become an irreplaceable intelligence resource that every faction must negotiate with for territorial operations across Tokyo Prefecture
Determine whether the collective consciousness entity can be communicated with or controlled through the memory fragments it consumes, and decide whether to destroy it or harness it

Determine whether the collective consciousness entity can be communicated with or controlled through the memory fragments it consumes, and decide whether to destroy it or harness it

provenance.worldgenOrigin: Maps Void Scar formations across Tokyo and exchanges collected civilian memory fragments for protective talismans.

loreCards.definition: A dense metropolitan grid in Japan's Kantō region housing over 13 million residents alongside covert populations of jujutsu sorcerers and independent chakra practitioners. Territorial disputes between the two power systems generate 47 documented Void Scars that erase civilian memories and feed a growing collective consciousness entity beneath the Yamanote Line.

A hidden campus within Tokyo Prefecture featuring traditional Japanese architecture, training grounds, and dormitories concealed behind Master Tengen's protective barrier. Conservative higher-ups and the Big Three Sorcerer Families govern sorcerer operations from this location, which faces escalating cursed spirit threats from chakra practitioner territorial disputes.

A mountainous prefecture northeast of Tokyo where independent chakra practitioners train in hidden camps without institutional oversight. Forested slopes and rocky outcroppings bear scorch marks, frost patterns, and impact craters from elemental chakra techniques, while overlap zones with jujutsu sorcerers produce cursed spirit infestations.

Repurposed tunnels and basement chambers beneath Tochigi Prefecture containing concrete fighting pits where jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners settle territorial disputes through regulated hand-to-hand combat. Kinji Hakari operates the venue as neutral ground, with staff redirecting Void Scars away from civilian areas above.

A secluded mountain campus in Japan's Kansai region dominated by traditional wooden temple architecture and enclosed stone training yards bordered by ancestral shrines of the Big Three Sorcerer Families. The school competes with Tokyo Jujutsu High for institutional dominance while investigating Void Scars appearing in Shikoku and Chūgoku regions.

A coastal prefecture south of Tokyo encompassing Yokohama port city, the Tanzawa mountain range, and dense residential suburbs connected to the capital by rail lines. Jujutsu sorcerer patrols encounter chakra practitioner communities in mountain passes, producing fog banks smelling of rotting flowers where strengthened cursed spirits concentrate.

A network of caverns and collapsed subway tunnels beneath the Yamanote Line railway containing a pulsating mass of accumulated civilian memories that registers on seismological equipment as a growing anomaly. Cursed spirits cluster around the entity to feed on concentrated negative emotions while its expansion causes structural instability in railway tunnels above.

Forty-seven documented reality fractures created by combat collisions between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners across Tokyo Prefecture. Each Void Scar erases one random memory from every civilian who passes through, feeding the collective consciousness entity beneath the Yamanote Line.

Seismologists detected a growing anomalous mass beneath the Yamanote Line railway formed from accumulated civilian memories harvested by Void Scars. The entity produces psychic static that triggers nosebleeds in subway passengers passing through adjacent stations.

Repeated elemental chakra technique training by independent practitioners permanently damaged Tochigi Prefecture's mountainous terrain, leaving scorch marks, frost patterns, and impact craters across forested slopes and rocky outcroppings. The visible environmental damage marks territories where chakra users refuse institutional oversight.

Suspended Tokyo Jujutsu High student Kinji Hakari constructed an underground fight club in repurposed tunnels beneath Tochigi Prefecture to serve as neutral ground for jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners. The venue facilitates regulated hand-to-hand combat matches that settle territorial disputes without generating uncontrolled Void Scars in civilian areas.

Kyoto Jujutsu High developed specialized anti-chakra engagement protocols based on twelve documented combat encounters with independent chakra practitioners. The protocols support Kyoto's campaign to classify chakra communities as hostile entities requiring forced registration and containment.

Tokyo Jujutsu High operates a hidden campus within Tokyo Prefecture protected by Master Tengen's barrier network, training sorcerers in cursed techniques under conservative higher-ups and the Big Three Sorcerer Families. The institution seeks to map and seal all 47 documented Void Scars, investigate the Yamanote Void Heart entity, and establish territorial boundaries with independent chakra practitioner communities.

Cursed Spirits manifest from negative emotions generated by territorial disputes between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners, concentrating in fog banks smelling of rotting flowers across Tokyo Prefecture's overlap zones. They seek to expand the Void Scar network beyond 47 documented sites, feed the Yamanote Void Heart collective consciousness with erased civilian memories, and prevent cooperation between sorcerers and chakra users.

The Gojo Clan wields political influence through three generations of accumulated debts, marriage alliances, and appointed loyalists embedded throughout Tokyo Jujutsu High's administrative hierarchy. The clan seeks to block formal accommodation treaties with chakra practitioners, secure three key advisory council positions, and maintain Gojo Satoru's operational autonomy without requiring higher-up approval.

Kyoto Jujutsu High operates from a secluded mountain campus in the Kansai region with faculty and students trained in anti-chakra engagement protocols developed from 12 documented combat encounters. The school seeks to classify independent chakra communities as hostile entities requiring forced registration, deploy sorcerer squads to neutralize Tochigi Prefecture training grounds, and establish a permanent monitoring station in Kanagawa Prefecture.

Twenty-three active field sorcerers operate across Tokyo and Tochigi Prefectures with direct combat experience against empowered curses and territorial chakra users, supplemented by Kinji Hakari's fight club intelligence network. An informal coalition of twelve sorcerers who survived Void Scar memory erosion advocates for cooperative engagement with chakra communities against the Yamanote Void Heart entity.

The advisory council governs sorcerer operations across both Tokyo and Kyoto Jujutsu High institutions through voting blocs controlled by the Big Three Sorcerer Families. The Kamo Clan guarantees three votes for conservative policy measures, requiring Kyoto Jujutsu High to secure majority support before classifying independent chakra communities as hostile entities.

Forty-seven documented Void Scars across Tokyo Prefecture each erase one random memory from every civilian who passes through their reality fracture perimeter. Combat collisions between cursed energy and elemental chakra techniques generate new Void Scars that feed the collective consciousness entity growing beneath the Yamanote Line railway.

Contested areas where jujutsu sorcerer and chakra practitioner territories intersect produce concentrated negative emotions that strengthen cursed spirits beyond either power system's suppression capacity. Atmospheric pressure fluctuations in these overlap zones trigger nosebleeds and unexplained anxiety in civilians passing through the affected areas.

Chakra practitioners across Japan's prefectures practice ninjutsu, genjutsu, and taijutsu as independent communities that refuse institutional oversight from Jujutsu High or any governing authority. Mountain training grounds in Tochigi Prefecture bear scorch marks, frost patterns, and impact craters from elemental chakra techniques used without centralized regulation.

The Gojo Clan, Kamo Clan, and Zenin Clan control hereditary cursed techniques and voting blocs within the conservative higher-ups' advisory structure that determines institutional policy. Their ancestral shrines border Kyoto Jujutsu High's training yards, and their accumulated political debts span three generations of administrative appointments and marriage alliances.

Special Grade jujutsu sorcerer and instructor at Tokyo Jujutsu High wielding the inherited Six Eyes and Limitless techniques. He trains first-year students Itadori Yuji, Megumi Fushiguro, and Nobara Kugisaki to reform the corrupt jujutsu establishment from within.

First-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High who became the unwilling vessel of Ryomen Sukuna after swallowing one of the Curse King's fingers. His superhuman physical strength makes him devastating in close combat while carrying the constant risk of Sukuna seizing control.

First-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High and designated heir of the Zenin Clan through his inherited Ten Shadows technique. He summons divine shikigami by channeling cursed energy through shadows while maintaining the conviction that saving others requires no calculation of worth.

First-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High who left her rural village to pursue sorcery in Tokyo and reunite with her childhood friend Saori. Her Straw Doll Technique channels cursed energy through nails and a hammer to strike curses with resonance attacks.

The King of Curses, an ancient sorcerer turned immortal cursed spirit whose fragmented fingers are scattered across Japan. One finger resides within Itadori Yuji's body, and the territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra users feed his gradual recovery of strength.

Special grade cursed spirit born from human hatred who wields Idle Transfiguration to reshape souls and bodies on contact. The territorial disputes between chakra users and sorcerers produce concentrated negative emotions that accelerate his power growth and supply fresh subjects for transfiguration research.

Suspended third-year student from Tokyo Jujutsu High who operates an underground gambling fight club in Tochigi Prefecture where sorcerers and chakra practitioners battle for high-stakes wagers. His Idle Death Gamble domain expansion uses pachinko mechanics to grant him temporary immortality through a jackpot system.

Immortal jujutsu sorcerer who has lived for over one thousand years, residing in the Tombs of the Star Corridor beneath Tokyo Jujutsu High. Their barrier technique anchors the hidden infrastructure of every major jujutsu installation in Japan and detects fluctuations caused by territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra users.

Former elite chakra operative who trains independent practitioners in ninjutsu and taijutsu from a concealed dojo in Tokyo Prefecture outside Jujutsu High's authority. His transplanted Sharingan eye allows him to analyze and replicate both chakra techniques and cursed techniques on sight.

Advanced sentient cursed corpse created by Principal Yaga Masamichi and second-year student at Tokyo Jujutsu High possessing three interchangeable cores. His multi-core architecture grants distinct combat forms including a standard panda body and gorilla mode, allowing him to survive damage that would destroy standard cursed corpses.

Principal of Tokyo Jujutsu High and master cursed corpse fabricator whose greatest creation Panda serves as living proof that constructed beings possess genuine consciousness. His administrative authority over mission assignments makes him the primary gatekeeper between student sorcerers and advanced training resources required for overlap zone operations.

Head of the Kedōin Clan, a small family of chakra practitioners that survived into modern Japan by cultivating medical ninjutsu techniques while refusing formal allegiance to any institutional authority. His medical supply operation in Tochigi Prefecture positions him as the primary broker between independent chakra communities and jujutsu society members seeking specialized remedies.

Semi-grade 1 sorcerer and instructor at Kyoto Jujutsu High who specializes in ritual-based cursed techniques that amplify allied sorcerers' abilities through song. Her ritual techniques maintain Kyoto's protective barriers against atmospheric pressure fluctuations caused by chakra-cursed energy collisions bleeding into Kansai region territories.

Independent cartographer who operates from the Yamanote Void Heart, surveying the 47 documented Void Scars across Tokyo Prefecture to track expansion patterns and the collective consciousness entity feeding on erased civilian memories. His survey data makes him the only reliable source of Void Scar intelligence for both Gojo Satoru's students and Kakashi Hatake's chakra practitioners.

Inherited ocular technique exclusive to the Gojo bloodline that grants the user perfect cursed energy perception and computational processing of spatial information. Gojo Satoru uses Six Eyes to optimize his Limitless technique with zero wasted cursed energy.

Spiritual energy generated by negative human emotions that jujutsu sorcerers channel into offensive and defensive techniques. Cursed spirits feed on this energy, growing stronger as territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra practitioners produce concentrated negative emotional output.

An independent power system encompassing ninjutsu for elemental manipulation, genjutsu for illusion techniques, and taijutsu for physical combat arts. Chakra practitioners refuse institutional oversight from jujutsu authorities, maintaining hidden training grounds in mountain territories across Japan's prefectures.

Reality fractures created when cursed energy and chakra techniques collide in combat, with 47 documented sites across Tokyo Prefecture alone. Each Void Scar erases one random memory from every civilian who passes through, feeding harvested memories to the collective consciousness entity beneath the Yamanote Line.

Territorial boundaries where jujutsu sorcerer and chakra practitioner operations intersect, producing atmospheric pressure fluctuations that trigger nosebleeds and unexplained anxiety in nearby civilians. Fog banks smelling of rotting flowers manifest in these zones, concentrating cursed energy and spawning new spirits.

Three aristocratic lineages—the Gojo, Kamo, and Zenin clans—that control institutional governance over jujutsu sorcerer society through political debts, marriage alliances, and appointed loyalists embedded in the conservative higher-ups' advisory structure. Each clan maintains inherited cursed technique libraries passed through their bloodlines.

An inherited cursed technique passed through the Gojo Clan that manipulates spatial infinity at the atomic level for offense and defense. Gojo Satoru combines this technique with his Domain Expansion: Unlimited Void to create guaranteed-hit effects.

A pinnacle cursed technique that creates a closed barrier environment embedding the user's innate domain with guaranteed-hit effects. Documented domains include Unlimited Void, Malevolent Shrine, Chimera Shadow Garden, Idle Death Gamble, and Self-Embodiment of Perfection.

Mahito's innate technique that reshapes souls and bodies on contact, converting humans into cursed corpses or altering physical forms with a touch. This soul manipulation renders Mahito immune to physical attacks, requiring direct cursed energy contact to inflict damage.

An inherited cursed technique passed through the Zenin Clan that summons divine shikigami by channeling cursed energy through shadows. Megumi Fushiguro wields this technique alongside his Domain Expansion: Chimera Shadow Garden.

Nobara Kugisaki's cursed technique that channels cursed energy through nails and a hammer to inflict resonance damage on curses. The technique establishes sympathetic connections between a straw doll proxy and the target.

A transplanted ocular ability possessed by Kakashi Hatake that analyzes and replicates both chakra techniques and cursed techniques on sight. Kakashi conceals this capability from Gojo Satoru and the jujutsu establishment to avoid being classified as a territorial threat.

Ryomen Sukuna's innate technique enabling devastating slashing attacks that cut through targets with guaranteed effect. These attacks operate within his Malevolent Shrine domain, which creates an unavoidable killing field unmatched in jujutsu history.

Kinji Hakari's Domain Expansion that operates through pachinko mechanics, granting him temporary immortality when the jackpot activates. This gambling-based technique makes Hakari nearly unbeatable in hand-to-hand combat when his luck holds.

Master Tengen's primary ability that conceals Tokyo Jujutsu High and sustains the Tombs of the Star Corridor beneath the school grounds. The barrier network extends through every major jujutsu installation in Japan, detecting fluctuations caused by territorial disputes between sorcerers and chakra users.

A technique mastered by Principal Yaga Masamichi that constructs sentient beings from cursed energy, with Panda serving as living proof that constructed entities possess genuine consciousness. Advanced cursed corpses like Panda utilize multi-core architectures granting distinct combat forms including gorilla mode.

Territorial disputes between jujutsu sorcerers and chakra practitioners generate concentrated negative emotions that strengthen cursed spirits beyond what either power system can suppress alone. Neither faction can eliminate the enhanced curses without the other's cooperation, forcing potential alliances despite institutional hostility.
